iv_application_how: Apply for the IV pension at your cantonal IV office. Fill out the application form and submit medical documents. A preliminary check is possible online.
iv_application_where: Find your IV office via the official website or contact the BSV (Federal Social Insurance Office).
iv_note: The IV pension is uniform at federal level. Payment is made monthly. Separate levels apply for HE (Helplessness Allowance).

nothilfe_application_guidance_title: Emergency Assistance – Important Note on Application
nothilfe_application_guidance_text: Emergency Assistance is a federal transitional solution (SEM). However, application and payment are handled by the cantonal migration authority or – in most cases – the social services of your Place of Residence Municipality. Please contact the social services of your municipality as soon as possible. Current contact details and further information can be found on your canton's website or at:
ipv_low_income_protection_note_suffix: % of premium subsidized) – final amount determined by SVA
nothilfe_note_2026: **Emergency Assistance 2026** (rules / amounts estimated for 2026) – mainly for persons with S Status, provisional admission (N status), or obligation to leave. The benefit consists mainly of benefits in kind (accommodation, meals, basic medical care, etc.), the cash component is usually low. The actual amount and form vary greatly depending on canton and municipality. Please contact your cantonal migration or social services for binding information.
nothilfe_note_realistic: The displayed amount is the estimated cash component. In most cantons, a large part of emergency assistance is provided as benefits in kind (accommodation, meals). The actual payout may be significantly lower.
nothilfe_note_transition: Emergency Assistance is a transitional solution. For longer stays or status changes (e.g. to F Permit (Provisional Admission) or B Permit (Residence Permit)), there is usually entitlement to Social Assistance (SH), which is significantly higher.
nothilfe_note_contact: The competent authority is usually the cantonal migration service or the municipal social services. Please contact your Place of Residence Municipality or the cantonal Office for Migration and Asylum.

step_grundbedarf_sh_note: Based on SKOS Guidelines C.3 (Version 1.1.2026): Single person 1061 CHF, couple 1624 CHF, +400 CHF per additional adult, children adjusted by age.
step_recognized_rent_sh_note: Maximum recognized rent according to EL (Supplementary Benefits) rent maxima 2026 (BSV), limited to regional tier. Legal basis: ELG Art. 10, SKOS Guidelines D.2.2.
step_health_extra_sh_note: Only with special medical needs. SKOS Guidelines D.3.1, up to 180 CHF per household.
step_integration_extra_sh_note: Integration Allowance (IZU) for employment or unemployment. SKOS Guidelines D.1.3, 100 CHF per adult.
step_other_expenses_sh_note: Additional expenses (e.g. medical, transport). Proof by receipts required. SKOS Guidelines D.4.
step_earned_deduction_sh_note: 20% deduction on earned income to promote employment. SKOS Guidelines C.2.2.
step_child_deduction_sh_note: Annual 3000 CHF deduction per child / youth in education. SKOS Guidelines C.2.3.
step_asset_consumption_sh_note: Excess assets are drawn down over 7 years (monthly attribution). SKOS Guidelines C.3.1, divisor 7. (Asset Drawdown)
step_other_benefits_deduction_sh_note: Deduction of benefits already received (IPV (Individual Premium Reduction)/EL (Supplementary Benefits)), as SH (Social Assistance) is subsidiary. Social Assistance Act AG § 15.

AI_ipv_income_definition_desc: Total taxable income + 10% total taxable assets + maintenance and administration costs for properties (over 20% lump sum deduction) + Pillar 3a contributions + purchase contributions to occupational pension plans + income according to BGSA + addition according to Art. 22ter and Art. 23 para. 1bis StG to 100%

AI_ipv_eligible_groups_desc: Persons subject to insurance with residence on January 1, in modest circumstances; total claim: jointly taxed households, cohabitation with child, young adults with parents (income < CHF 12'000)
AI_ipv_step_1_formula: LNA = taxable_income + 0.1 × taxable_assets + property_maintenance_excess + pillar_3a + pillar_2_buyins + bgsa_income + art_22_23_adjustments
AI_ipv_step_2_formula: totalRefPremium = numAdults × ref_adult + numYoungAdults × ref_young_adult + numChildren × ref_child
AI_ipv_step_3_desc: ③ Calculate deductible rate based on LNA
AI_ipv_step_5_desc: ⑤ Subsidy amount before Minimum Guarantee
AI_ipv_step_6_desc: ⑥ Apply increase to the minimum guarantee (for children/youth)
AI_ipv_step_7_desc: ⑦ Annual Premium Reduction (max. effective premium, min. CHF 100)
AI_ipv_step_7_formula: annualBenefit = max(100, min(enhancedSubsidy, annualHealthPremium)) if subsidy > 0 else 0
AI_ipv_formula_note: Variable deductible 7–12%. Minimum guarantee: 80% for children, 50% for youth in education if determining income (LNA) ≤ CHF 75,000. No asset limit, but 10% of assets added to income. Effective income cap due to high deductible.

BE_ipv_algorithm_description: Income-dependent tiered model with social deductions, asset surcharge (5%) and region-specific monthly amounts based on age and family circumstances
BE_ipv_tier_1_desc: Income Tier 1: up to CHF 9'000 Determining Income (LNA)
BE_ipv_tier_2_desc: Income Tier 2: up to CHF 17'000 Determining Income (LNA)
BE_ipv_tier_3_desc: Income Tier 3: up to CHF 25'000 Determining Income (LNA)
BE_ipv_tier_4_desc: Income Tier 4: up to CHF 35'000 Determining Income (LNA) (for singles)
BE_ipv_tier_5_desc: Income Tier 5: up to CHF 45'000 Determining Income (LNA) (for families with children)
BE_ipv_tier_none_desc: No Entitlement: Determining Income (LNA) over CHF 45'000 (families) or CHF 35'000 (singles)
BE_ipv_household_bonus_desc: No multipliers; instead social deductions for married couples (13'000), single parents (9'750), single persons (2'200) and per child (15'000 first, 12'500 second, 10'000 additional)
BE_ipv_child_bonus_desc: No percentage surcharges; child-specific social deductions and age-dependent monthly amounts
BE_ipv_step_1_formula: KRE = net_income + pillar_2/3a_contributions + dual_earner_deduction + other_non_taxable_income + membership_fees + external_weekly_stay + property_maintenance_difference + negative_net_yield_investments + loss_carried_forward - self-borne_medical_costs
BE_ipv_step_2_formula: KV = max(0, assets - 17'000 per family member)
BE_ipv_step_3_formula: ME = KRE + 5% KV - social_deductions (couple: 13'000, single_parent: 9'750, single: 2'200, child1: 15'000, child2: 12'500, additional_children: 10'000)
BE_ipv_step_4_desc: ④ Determine applicable income tier and premium region
BE_ipv_step_5_desc: ⑤ Read monthly subsidy per person based on age, education and region
BE_ipv_step_6_desc: ⑥ Calculate total annual amount (limited to effective premium)
BE_ipv_step_6_formula: annual_benefit = min(sum(monthly_subsidy_per_person * 12 for all members), annualHealthPremium)
BE_ipv_formula_note: Calculation based on tax data from the year before last; automatic check; manual applications for changes. No claims can be derived from simulation. For source-taxed persons: 75% gross income minus maintenance payments.

This is an Estimate based on SKOS 2026. Child needs are calculated in a simplified manner (average). Official calculation by the municipal social service.
FR_sozialhilfe_required_documents_list: ✓ Completed registration form from the regional social service
✓ Official ID (identity card or passport) of all household members
✓ Current rental agreement including the latest utility bill
✓ Health insurance policy and current premium statement
✓ Pay slips from the last 12 months for all employed persons
✓ Complete bank statements for all accounts from the last 6 months
✓ Latest final tax assessment and current tax return
✓ Proof of all other income (pensions, alimony, allowances)
FR_ipv_legal_basis_additional: Health Insurance Act (KGV) of the Canton of Fribourg
FR_ipv_algorithm_description: Progressive Smoothing Model with 60 Tiers: Subsidy rate (1-65%) based on the percentage distance of the Determining Income (LNA) from the cantonal income limit, applied to the cantonal average premium.
FR_ipv_income_definition_desc: Determining Income (LNA): Taxable income of the previous year + interest > CHF 30,000 + property costs > CHF 15,000 + 5% taxable assets - maintenance contributions - CHF 5,000 per child - 100% family allowances. For individuals taxed at source: 80% gross salary + 5% assets.
FR_ipv_income_period_desc: Tax assessment of the previous year (reference date 31.08. of the following year)
FR_ipv_eligible_groups_desc: Children/young adults with parents; cohabiting couples with child; married couples; single parents; individuals taxed at source.
FR_ipv_step_1_formula: LNA = taxable_income + 0.05 × taxable_assets - (5000 × numChildren) - maintenance_contributions - family_allowances
FR_ipv_step_2_desc: ② Determine income limit based on family constellation
FR_ipv_step_3_desc: ③ Calculate distance to limit in % and read smoothing rate (1-65%)
FR_ipv_step_4_desc: ④ Calculate subsidy per category (min. rate children 80%, young adults 50%)
FR_ipv_step_4_formula: subsidy = ref_adult × rate + ref_young × max(rate, 0.5) + ref_child × max(rate, 0.8)
FR_ipv_final_step_desc: ⑤ Determine final subsidy (max. effective premium)
FR_ipv_formula_note: Smoothing with 60 Tiers (1-65%). Min. 80% for children, min. 50% for young adults in education. Regional average premiums.
FR_ipv_application_method: Online via Guichet Unique; Upon request to the Compensation Office; Potential recipients receive a form.
FR_ipv_application_authority: Compensation Office Fribourg; Public Health Service
FR_ipv_contact_address: Impasse de la Colline 1, Case postale, 1700 Fribourg

✓ Copy of the current health insurance policy for all household members
✓ Final tax assessment of the previous year (or provisional, if not yet available)
✓ Copy of a valid ID (ID card, passport or residence permit)
✓ Current salary certificates of the last 3 months (if employed)
✓ Proof of pensions or allowances (e.g., AHV, IV, family allowances)
✓ Proof of education for young adults (18-25 years) in education/training
✓ Proof of alimony payments made or received
✓ Rental contract or proof of housing costs (for rent or ownership)
✓ Bank details (IBAN) for payment of subsidies

✓ Copies of the birth certificates of all children

FR_fa_special_notes: In the Canton of Fribourg, graduated family allowances apply: higher rates from the third child onwards. Birth and adoption allowances are CHF 1,500 each.

FR_unterhalt_notes: Reference: Bernese Child Support Calculation (BUB). Often used as a guideline in the bilingual canton of FR. Values are approximations, court practice may differ.
GE_sh_region_warning: The region assignment is based on postal code data (Swiss Post), which may not 100% align with cantonal guidelines. For accurate values, contact your social service office. In the Canton of Geneva, uniform urban rent references apply; no regional subdivision.
GE_sh_employment_note: The Integration Allowance (IZU) and Income Exemption (EFB) apply only in case of employment (employmentStatus='employed'). If not specified, 'other' is assumed and no allowances are granted.
GE_sh_disclaimer_note: This is an Estimate. For a binding calculation, contact the official social service office of your municipality. Our accuracy is approx. 90-95%, as local variations are possible.

GE_ipv_legal_basis_additional: Cantonal Application Act of the Federal Health Insurance Act (LaLAMal) (J 3 10)
GE_ipv_algorithm_description: Income group model: Fixed subsidy per adult according to income group (G1-G9) + child bonus per child/young adult.
GE_ipv_income_definition_desc: Unified Determining Income (RDU): Taxable income + Pillar 3a + 25% taxable assets + >1% property value + illness/disability costs >CHF 5,000 - maintenance contributions. For individuals taxed at source: 70% gross salary + 25% assets.
GE_ipv_income_period_desc: Tax period N-2 (previous year's tax assessment for the following year)
GE_ipv_eligible_groups_desc: Children/young adults with parents; cohabiting couples with child; married couples; single parents; individuals taxed at source; persons without taxation.
GE_ipv_step_1_desc: ① Calculate annual Unified Determining Income (RDU)
GE_ipv_step_2_desc: ② Determine income group (G1-G9) based on RDU
GE_ipv_step_3_desc: ③ Calculate subsidy per adult based on group
GE_ipv_step_4_desc: ④ Add child bonus per child/young adult
GE_ipv_step_5_desc: ⑤ Determine final premium reduction (max. effective premium, min. CHF 53/month if children present)
GE_ipv_formula_note: No application deadline. Automatic calculation for assessed persons. Minimum subsidy (only with children): CHF 53/month. Young adults 19-25: separate calculation possible. Max. RDU CHF 151,000. Max. assets CHF 500,000.
GE_ipv_application_method: Online via Geneva Subsidy (automatic for assessed persons); Paper form for individuals taxed at source/without data; Affidavit possible.
GE_ipv_application_authority: Canton of Geneva; SAM (Geneva Health Insurance Service)

✓ If applicable: proof of illness or disability costs (if exceeding CHF 5,000)
✓ If applicable: proof of alimony payments made or received

GL_ipv_legal_basis_additional: Act on the Introduction of the Federal Health Insurance Act (EG KVG GL)
GL_ipv_algorithm_description: Deductible model: Subsidy = Reference premium – graduated deductible (9–14% of determining income (LNA) after basic and child deductions).
GL_ipv_income_definition_desc: Determining Income (LNA): Gross income + 10% taxable assets + property losses – imputed rental value – maintenance contributions. For individuals taxed at source: application required. Persons without taxation: 70% gross salary + 1/15 assets.
GL_ipv_income_period_desc: Tax data N-2 (previous year's tax assessment for the following year)
GL_ipv_eligible_groups_desc: Children/young adults with parents; cohabiting couples with child; married couples; single parents; individuals taxed at source; social assistance and supplementary benefits recipients.
GL_ipv_step_1_formula: LNA = gross_income + 0.1 × taxable_assets + property_losses - own_rental_value - maintenance_contributions
GL_ipv_step_2_desc: ② Determine Basic Needs and Child Deduction
GL_ipv_step_3_desc: ③ Determine deductible rate and calculate deductible amount
GL_ipv_step_3_formula: excess = max(0, LNA - basic_deduction - child_deduction); retention_rate = lookup(excess); selfRetentionAmount = excess × retention_rate
GL_ipv_step_5_desc: ⑤ Determine final premium reduction (max. effective premium, min. CHF 100)
GL_ipv_formula_note: Application deadline 31.01.2026 (late applications: from the following month). Children: minimum reduction 80%, young adults in education 50%. EL/Social assistance: 100% guideline premium. Max. assets: CHF 100,000 (individual)/CHF 160,000 (couples). Change ±30%: recalculation.

GR_sh_disclaimer_note: This is an **estimate** based on cantonal guidelines and SKOS 2026. The actual benefit may vary. For a binding calculation, please contact the social welfare authority of your municipality of residence.

GR_ipv_legal_basis_additional: Cantonal Introductory Act to the Federal Health Insurance Act (EGzKVG GR)
GR_ipv_algorithm_description: Deductible model: Subsidy = Reference premium – graduated deductible (5–10% of the excess after deductions). Special child relief when LNA ≤ CHF 80,000.
GR_ipv_income_definition_desc: Determining Income (LNA): Taxable income + 10% net assets + non-taxed income + net property income (negative) + Pillar 2 & 3a contributions + donations – maintenance contributions.
GR_ipv_income_period_desc: Previous year (tax data N-1). The application deadline ends strictly on 31.12. of the application year.
GR_ipv_eligible_groups_desc: Families; single parents; married couples; individuals taxed at source; EL/social assistance recipients; cross-border commuters under KVG.
GR_ipv_step_1_desc: ① Calculate annual Determining Income (LNA) including addition of pension contributions
GR_ipv_step_1_formula: LNA = taxable_income + 0.1 × taxable_assets + pillar_2_3a + contributions + non_taxed_income - alimony
GR_ipv_step_2_desc: ② Determine basic deduction (32k/48k) and child deduction (9k)
GR_ipv_step_3_desc: ③ Determine excess income and progressive deductible (5-10%)
GR_ipv_step_3_formula: excess = max(0, LNA - total_deductions); retention_rate = lookup_rate(excess)
GR_ipv_step_4_desc: ④ Calculate subsidy including correction factor for children (LNA-dependent)
GR_ipv_step_5_desc: ⑤ Determine final premium reduction (max. effective premium, min. CHF 100)
GR_ipv_formula_note: Attention: Application deadline 31.12.2026. Child guarantee: Full relief if LNA < CHF 65,000.

JU_ipv_algorithm_description: Tier model: Fixed subsidy per person according to income level (in steps of 1,000) + child bonus + family supplement for low earned income.
JU_ipv_income_definition_desc: Determining Income (LNA): Taxable income + 5% taxable assets - maintenance contributions. For individuals taxed at source: gross salary of the current year if not taxed. Cohabiting couples with child: cumulative income. No consideration of Pillar 2 buy-ins and Pillar 3a contributions.
JU_ipv_income_period_desc: Tax assessment of the previous year (tax return 2024 for 2026); current year upon request.
JU_ipv_eligible_groups_desc: Children/young adults with parents; cohabiting couples with child (cumulated); married couples; single parents; individuals taxed at source; EL/SA recipients.
JU_ipv_step_2_desc: ② Determine income level (steps of 1,000) based on LNA
JU_ipv_step_3_desc: ③ Determine basic subsidy based on level
JU_ipv_step_4_desc: ④ Add child bonus per child/young adult
JU_ipv_step_5_desc: ⑤ Add family supplement for low earned income
JU_ipv_step_6_desc: ⑥ Determine final premium reduction (max. effective premium, min. CHF 120 if children present)
JU_ipv_step_6_formula: annualBenefit = max(min(baseSubsidy + childBonus + familySupplement, annualHealthPremium), minSubsidy if hasChildren)
JU_ipv_formula_note: No application deadline. Children/young adults in education: reduced rate. Max. income CHF 52,999 (families) or CHF 26,999 (individuals). Asset limit CHF 150,000. Active parents: supplement max. CHF 3,600 / min. CHF 180 (RDU <18,000).

NE_ipv_algorithm_description: Classification system S1 to S15 based on the Determining Income (LNA) (revenu déterminant: effective income from tax return item 5.5 excl. imputed rental value - permitted deductions + 30% effective assets after allowances) and family composition. Fixed monthly subsidies per classification and age category (Enfants, Jeunes adultes en formation, Jeunes adultes, Adultes en formation, Adultes). Amounts adjusted proportionally to premium increases per age category. Mixed Automaticity from January 2026: Automatic renewal for existing recipients; quasi-automatic (personalized letter with Reply Coupon) for new eligible persons via tax data.
NE_ipv_income_definition_desc: Determining Income (LNA) (revenu déterminant): Effective income (tax return item 5.5, column Revenu, excl. value of private rent item 4.1) - permitted deductions (AHV/AI contributions item 6.10, alimony payments item 6.7, occupational costs real max. CHF 10,000 item 6.4 + ancillary costs CHF 2,400 item 6.5) + 30% effective assets (tax return item 6.16, column Fortune, after deductions: CHF 4,000 for single persons, CHF 8,000 for couples, CHF 2,000 per minor child, max. CHF 10,000 per household). For source-taxed persons: gross salary of the current year, if not taxed.
NE_ipv_income_period_desc: Tax assessment post numerando of the previous year (current tax year, cut-off date 31.12. of the following year). For 2026 based on tax return 2025.
NE_ipv_eligible_groups_desc: Children/young adults with parents; cohabitation with child; married couples; single parents; source-taxed persons; refugees; EL/AS recipients; young adults 19-25 years (even without education)
NE_ipv_step_1_formula: LNA = effective income (item 5.5 excl. 4.1) - deductions (items 6.4 max 10,000 + 6.5 2,400 + 6.7 + 6.10) + 0.3 × (assets item 6.16 - allowances: 4,000 single / 8,000 couple / 2,000 per child max 10,000)
NE_ipv_step_2_desc: ② Determine family composition and number of children
NE_ipv_step_2_formula: family_type = determineFamilyType(numAdults, numEducation, isMarried); childrenCount = min(numChildren, 10)
NE_ipv_step_3_desc: ③ Assign Classification S1-S15 based on LNA
NE_ipv_step_4_desc: ④ Calculate fixed monthly subsidy per category
NE_ipv_step_4_formula: monthlySubsidy = numAdults * subsidyTables.adultes[classification] + numEducation * subsidyTables.jeunes_adultes_en_formation[classification] + numChildren * subsidyTables.enfants[classification]
NE_ipv_step_5_desc: ⑤ Apply Minimum Reductions and caps
NE_ipv_step_5_formula: annualSubsidy = max(monthlySubsidy * 12, minForChildren + minForYoung, 100); final = min(annualSubsidy, annualHealthPremium); For deductible option: proportional reduction
NE_ipv_step_6_desc: ⑥ Determine Final Premium Reduction (max. effective premium, min. CHF 100)
NE_ipv_step_6_formula: annualBenefit = finalAnnual; For social assistance/EL/PC-AVS/AI: special amounts (e.g., 687 for Adultes)
NE_ipv_formula_note: No fixed deadline (application anytime, retroactive max. 5 years). Children/young adults in education: 80%/50% Minimum Reduction. Minimum Subsidy CHF 100. For income below 15,000 single/20,000 couple +3,000 per child: special application at GSR (Regional Social Counter NE). Children: OSL Guarantee (S1-S15); Young adults in education S1-S13: OSL Guarantee; S14-S15: Smoothing to avoid threshold effects.
NE_ipv_application_method: Application at the GSR (Regional Social Counter NE) of the municipality of residence. Mixed Automaticity from 2026: Automatic renewal for existing recipients; new eligible persons receive a personalized letter with Reply Coupon (return within 30 days). Application possible at any time in case of change of situation or special cases (e.g., 19-25 years, source-taxed persons, self-employed).

OW_ipv_algorithm_description: Deductible model: Subsidy = Reference Premium - deductible (9.5% of countable income, staggered from CHF 35,000 by +0.01% per CHF 100)
OW_ipv_income_definition_desc: Countable income: Total of income (tax return item 1990) - occupational expenses - maintenance contributions - insurance deduction - illness/accident/disability costs - childcare costs - mortgage interest (up to max. property income) - CHF 7,000 deduction for married couples - CHF 7,000 per child + any property losses + 10% taxable assets. Source-taxed persons: 75% of gross earned income subject to source tax for the year 2024
OW_ipv_income_period_desc: Definitive tax assessment 2024 (or 2025 for new arrivals or changes in tax liability)
OW_ipv_eligible_groups_desc: Persons with primary tax residence in Obwalden on January 1, 2026; Compulsorily insured with a recognized health insurance fund; Fulfillment of economic requirements or receipt of social assistance/Supplementary Benefits; Young adults (born 2008 onwards) submit their own application
OW_ipv_step_1_formula: countable_income = total_income - deductions (occupational_expenses + maintenance_contributions + insurance_deduction + illness_costs + childcare + mortgage_interest max. property_income + 7000 couples + 7000 per child) + property_losses + 0.1 × taxable_assets
OW_ipv_step_3_desc: ③ Calculate deductible (9.5% base + staggered from 35,000)
OW_ipv_step_5_desc: ⑤ Determine Final Premium Reduction (max. effective premium, incl. Minimum Guarantees)
OW_ipv_step_5_formula: annualBenefit = min(calculatedSubsidy, annualHealthPremium); annualBenefit = max(annualBenefit, minGuarantee)
OW_ipv_formula_note: Minimum Guarantee: Children 80% of Reference Premium with income <50,000 (100% from 4th child); Young adults 50% with income <25,000. Source-taxed persons must actively apply. No Entitlement if countable income >50,000 (without children)/>70,000 (with children).

SG_ipv_algorithm_description: Dynamic affordability limit model: Subsidy = Reference Premium – affordability rate × Determining Income (LNA), with progressive adjustment from income threshold and Minimum Guarantee for children
SG_ipv_income_definition_desc: Determining Income (LNA): Net income - rental value etc. +20% assets + additions (Pillar 3a etc.) – Child Deduction CHF 4,000
SG_ipv_eligible_groups_desc: Children/young adults with parents; cohabitation with child; married couples; new residents (purchasing power adjustment); social assistance and EL recipients (new from 2026 due to federal regulation)
SG_ipv_step_3_desc: ③ Determine income threshold by household type and number of children
SG_ipv_step_4_desc: ④ Calculate dynamic affordability rate (base 9.2% + increase)
SG_ipv_step_5_desc: ⑤ Check Minimum Guarantee for children/young adults
SG_ipv_step_6_desc: ⑥ Calculate Annual Premium Reduction (max. effective premium)
SG_ipv_step_6_formula: annualBenefit = min(max(totalRefPremium - LNA × dynamicRate, minSubsidy), annualHealthPremium, totalRefPremium)
SG_ipv_formula_note: Minimum Subsidy CHF 100. If income >20% above threshold: no subsidy. EL and social assistance: full Reference Premium (new from 2026 due to federal regulation).

SH_ipv_algorithm_description: Deductible model: Subsidy = Reference Premium – 15% × (Determining Income (LNA) after basic and child deductions); Subsidy < 100 CHF = 0 CHF
SH_ipv_income_definition_desc: Assessable income: Net income + 15% net assets - Basic Deduction (4,500/9,000 CHF) - Child Deduction (4,500 CHF per child)

SH_ipv_step_4_desc: ④ Calculate annual premium reduction (max. actual premium, <100 CHF = 0)
SH_ipv_step_4_formula: preSubsidy = max(0, totalRefPremium - incomeDeduction); annualBenefit = preSubsidy < 100 ? 0 : min(preSubsidy, annualHealthPremium)
SH_ipv_formula_note: Amounts below CHF 100 are not paid out. Children/young adults in education: Minimum Reduction (depending on income). Max. income approx. CHF 80,000. Deadline: April 30 of the following year.

SO_ipv_step_5_formula: preSubsidy = max(0, totalRefPremium - (max(0, LNA - deductions) * rate))

SZ_ipv_algorithm_description: Income percentage model: Subsidy = Reference Premium – 11% × (assessable income after child deduction). Capped at actual premium. Minimum guarantee: 80% of the reference premium for children and 50% for young adults in education.
SZ_ipv_income_definition_desc: Assessable income: Net income (from tax return) + 10% assets (after lump-sum deduction: CHF 25,000 per adult + CHF 15,000 per child/young adult in education) + add-backs (e.g., property maintenance).
SZ_ipv_income_period_desc: Latest legally valid tax assessment (reference date Dec 31 of the following year). For 2026 based on the 2025 assessment.
SZ_ipv_eligible_groups_desc: Children/young adults with parents; cohabitation with child; married couples; single parents; source-taxed individuals; refugees; EL/SH recipients
SZ_ipv_step_1_formula: LNA = net_income + 0.1 × max(0, assets - exemption adult 25,000 × numAdults - exemption child 15,000 × (numChildren + numEducation)) + add_backs
SZ_ipv_step_2_desc: ② Subtract child deduction from assessable income (CHF 5,000 per child/young adult)
SZ_ipv_step_3_formula: totalRefPremium = numAdults × standardPremiumAdult + numEducation × standardPremiumYoung + numChildren × standardPremiumChild
SZ_ipv_step_4_desc: ④ Calculate deductible (11% of adjusted LNA)
SZ_ipv_step_5_desc: ⑤ Calculate basic subsidy and apply Minimum Guarantee
SZ_ipv_step_5_formula: baseSubsidy = totalRefPremium - deductible; subsidy = max(baseSubsidy, minChildren + minYoung)
SZ_ipv_step_6_desc: ⑥ Determine final premium reduction (capped at actual premium, min. CHF 100)
SZ_ipv_step_6_formula: annualBenefit = min(max(subsidy, 100), annualHealthPremium); For optional deductible: proportional reduction
SZ_ipv_formula_note: Children/young adults in education: minimum guarantee of 80%/50% of the reference premium. Variable maximum income (approx. CHF 95,980 as example). No strict asset limit. Automatic for potential/previous year entitled persons.

TG_ipv_algorithm_description: Tax amount-based category system: Fixed amounts based on simple tax-determining rate at 100% (Categories A/B/C for adults, D for children). No entitlement if taxable assets >0 CHF. Pure application principle.
TG_ipv_income_definition_desc: Calculation basis: Simple tax-determining rate at 100% (in CHF). No direct income calculation; categories based on tax amount (e.g., up to 400 Fr. for A). Taxable assets ≤0 CHF.
TG_ipv_income_period_desc: Current tax assessment (provisional previous year, final following year, reference date Dec 31 of the following year). For 2026 based on 2025 assessment.
TG_ipv_eligible_groups_desc: Children/young adults with parents; cohabitation with child; married couples; single parents; refugees; EL/SH recipients
TG_ipv_step_1_desc: ① Check tax amount and assets
TG_ipv_step_1_formula: If taxable assets >0, then no entitlement. Otherwise category based on simple tax at 100% (e.g., ≤400 Fr. = A)
TG_ipv_step_2_desc: ② Assign category (A/B/C for adults, D for children)
TG_ipv_step_2_formula: category = lookupTaxAmount(tax_amount); For children always D
TG_ipv_step_3_desc: ③ Calculate fixed subsidy per category
TG_ipv_step_4_formula: subsidy = max(subsidy, numChildren × childRef * 0.8 + numEducation × youngRef * 0.5)
TG_ipv_step_5_formula: annualBenefit = min(subsidy, annualHealthPremium); If <50 CHF, then 0
TG_ipv_step_6_formula: For optional deductible: proportional reduction; For social assistance/EL: special amounts
TG_ipv_formula_note: Pure application principle: Application required anew each year from the Place of Residence Municipality. Deadline December 31, 2026. Children/young adults in education: 80%/50% Minimum Reduction. Minimum subsidy CHF 50.
TG_ipv_application_method: The application must be submitted annually via the online portal (IPV-TG) or using a paper form to the Place of Residence Municipality.

TI_ipv_algorithm_description: Progressive LAPS model: RD (Disposable Income) = (Income + 30% Assets) - Expenses. Subsidy based on coverage rate of PMR (Reference Premium) according to RD levels.
TI_ipv_income_definition_desc: RD (Disposable Income TI): Net income + 30% × (assets - exemption) - LAPS basic needs - rent - insurance lump sum

TI_ipv_step_1_desc: ① Calculate RD (Disposable Income TI) considering 30% asset portion
TI_ipv_step_3_desc: ③ Determine progressive subsidy rate (λ) according to RD table 2026
TI_ipv_step_5_desc: ⑤ Annual premium reduction (≥ 120 CHF per person)

UR_ipv_algorithm_description: Income percentage model: Subsidy = MIN(Standard Premium – 9.75% × Determining Income (LNA), 65% × Standard Premium)
UR_ipv_income_definition_desc: PV Income: Net income + 15% assets - deductions (real estate etc.) - basic deduction (30k/55k) - CHF 3,500 per child
UR_ipv_income_period_desc: Reference date: January 1, 2026 (based on 2024 tax assessment)
UR_ipv_eligible_groups_desc: Children/young adults with parents; cohabitation with child; married couples; single parents; refugees; provisionally admitted persons
UR_ipv_step_1_desc: ① Calculate basic and child deductions from Determining Income (LNA)
UR_ipv_step_3_desc: ③ Calculate income deduction (9.75% of Determining Income (LNA))
UR_ipv_step_4_desc: ④ Calculate maximum subsidy amount (65% coverage limit)
UR_ipv_step_5_desc: ⑤ Determine annual premium reduction (minimum CHF 100)
UR_ipv_step_5_formula: annualBenefit = max(100, min(totalRefPremium - incomeDeduction, maxSubsidyCap, actualPremium))
UR_ipv_formula_note: Max. household income CHF 120,000. Asset limit CHF 300,000/450,000. Children/young adults: 80%/50% Minimum Guarantee.

VD_ipv_step_2_formula: Unified Determining Income (RDU) = Determining Income (LNA) – Exemption (56,000/112,000) – (Children × 15,000)
VD_ipv_step_3_desc: ③ Determine subsidy rate from RDU Tier / Level
VD_ipv_step_4_desc: ④ Determine the Calculated Subsidy Amount
VD_ipv_step_5_desc: ⑤ Annual Premium Reduction (max. effective premium)
VD_ipv_formula_note: Reference Premium: 6,520 CHF (Region 1) / 6,110 CHF (Region 2). Subsidy rates of the application year.

ZG_ipv_step_4_desc: ④ Calculate income deduction (8% of total income)

national_asylum_note: Asylum Seeker Allowance (Asylsozialhilfe) and Emergency Assistance (Nothilfe) in Switzerland are administered at the cantonal level, with significant differences between cantons. There are no nationally uniform amounts or calculation formulas. Benefits are generally lower than regular Social Assistance (SH), often 10-50% less, and are frequently provided as in-kind support (Sachleistungen) such as collective accommodation plus pocket money (Taschengeld). The federal government pays lump sums to the cantons, but actual benefits depend on cantonal regulations. For accurate, up-to-date information, please contact the local cantonal authority. The data is based on public sources; estimates are approximate and subject to change.
asyl_info_general_note: Benefits for asylum seekers and refugees vary greatly between cantons and are often designed as in-kind support (accommodation, meals) plus limited pocket money.
status_fs_info: For provisionally admitted persons (Status F) and persons in need of protection (Status S)
recognized_refugee_note: Recognized refugees are generally entitled to regular Social Assistance (SH), which is higher than Asylum Seeker Allowance.

children_may_increase: Support may be higher for children
